Numbered flagging tape Reason for Removal:OtherComments:This tree has been previously topped and now consists primarily of epicormic regrowth. A large fruiting body consistent with Ganoderma is
present at the base of the trunk. It is located within a cluster exhibiting a deformed structural root plate shared with adjacent trees
		
		
			Tree number:2Species:Pine Tree Size (DBH):16Tree location:See site map Description of Tree:Numbered flagging tape Reason for Removal:OtherComments:This tree exhibits an off-balance, asymmetrical canopy with loss of a central leader. The canopy extends over active vehicle parking and
pedestrian use areas and is in close proximity to adjacent dwellings. It shares a deformed structural root plate with adjacent trees
		
		
			Tree number:3Species:Sweetgum Tree Size (DBH):12Tree location:See site map Description of Tree:Numbered flagging tape Reason for Removal:OtherComments:This tree is growing in direct contact with an adjacent pine (Tree 4), with basal stems making contact with one another. The root systems are
deformed and part of a shared structural root plate with adjacent trees (Trees 1, 2, and 4), with conditions consistent with overlapping structural
root zones within the cluster. The tree exhibits poor form, and the central leader suggests a previous breakage event with subsequent
re-sprouting, resulting in weakly attached regrowth with poor taper and loss of apical dominance
		
		
			Tree number:4Species:Pine Tree Size (DBH):21Tree location:See site map Description of Tree:Numbered flagging tape Reason for Removal:OtherComments:This tree is co-dominant with Tree 3 and part of a multi-tree cluster (Trees 1, 2, and 3) exhibiting a deformed structural root plate. The root
systems are deformed with adjacent trees. Foliage appears sparse. Given the size and position of this tree within the cluster and existing target
opportunities, permitting other trees in the cluster raises structural root plate concerns. A minor rub wound is present with sap exudation; noted as
an identifying feature consistent with close stem proximity
		
		
			Tree number:5Species:Pine Tree Size (DBH):16Tree location:See site map Description of Tree:Numbered flagging tape Reason for Removal:OtherComments:This tree exhibits a pronounced lean/bow toward an adjacent apartment complex. Embedded wire is present at the lower stem, resulting in visible
girdling at the basal area.
		
		
			Tree number:6Species:Sweetgum Tree Size (DBH):15Tree location:See site map Description of Tree:Numbered flagging tape Reason for Removal:OtherComments:This tree has been previously topped and now consists primarily of epicormic regrowth
